definition:
1. to turn  or direct  towards a thing, (Homer), etc.; mostly followed by a preposition, τρ. τινὰ εἰς εὐνήν to shew  him to bed, (Odyssey by Homer); τρ. πόλεις ἐς ὕβριν (Thucydides); τρ. κεφαλὴν πρὸς ἠέλιον (Odyssey by Homer) 
2. Pass. to turn one's steps, turn in a certain direction , τραφθῆναι ἀν᾽ Ἑλλάδα to roam  up and down Greece, (Odyssey by Homer); with accusative cognate, τρέπεσθαι ὁδόν to take  a course, (Herdotus Historicus) 
3. Pass. also to turn  or betake oneself , εἰς ἀοιδήν (Odyssey by Homer); ἐπὶ ἔργα (Iliad by Homer); ἐφ᾽ ἁρπαγήν (Thucydides); πρὸς ληιστείαν (Thucydides) 
4. Pass. and Mid., of places, to be turned  or look  in a certain direction, πρὸς ζόφον (Odyssey by Homer); πρὸς ἄρκτον, πρὸς νότον (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
5. to turn , i. e. turn about , τρέπειν ἵππους (Iliad by Homer); τὰ καλὰ τρ. ἔζω to turn  the best side outmost, (Pindar):—;Pass., αἰχμὴ τράπετο the point bent back , (Iliad by Homer); of the solstice, ἐπειδὰν ἐν χειμῶνι τράπηται ἥλιος (see. τροπή I) (Xenophon Historicus) 
6. τρ. τὴν αἰτίαν, τὴν ὀργὴν εἴς τινα to divert  the blame, the anger upon another, (Isaeus Orator):—;Pass., in imprecations, ἐς κεφαλὴν τρέποιτο ἐμοί on my head be it! (Aristophanes Comicus) 
7. to turn another way, to alter, change , νόον, φρένας (Homer), etc.; ἐς γέλων τρ. τὸ πρᾶγμα (Aristophanes Comicus):—;Pass. to be changed, change , (Homer), etc.; with accusative cognate, τρεπόμενος τροπάς undergoing  changes, (Aeschines Orator) 
8. to turn  or put to flight, rout, defeat , (Iliad by Homer), etc.; τρ. φύγαδε, Lat. convertere in fugam, (Iliad by Homer); τρ. ἐς φυγήν (Euripides);—;so, in aor1 middle, to put an enemy to flight , (Xenophon Historicus):—;Pass. to be put to flight, turn and flee , (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc.; so in Mid., ἐς φυγὴν τραπέσθαι (Herdotus Historicus):—;also intransitive in Act., φύγαδ᾽ ἔτραπε (Iliad by Homer) 
9. to turn away, keep off, hinder , τρ. τινὰ ἀπὸ τείχεος (Iliad by Homer); βέλος ἔτραπεν ἄλληι (Iliad by Homer) 
10. to overturn , like ἀνατρέπω, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
11. to turn, apply , τρ. τι ἐς ἄλλο τι (Herdotus Historicus); ποῦ τέτροφας τὰς ἐμβάδα; what have you made of  your shoes? (Aristophanes Comicus):—;Pass., ποῖ τρέπεται τὰ χρήματ; (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)
